chunk_summary: This section discusses diagnostic cutoffs, clinical risk stratification, and laboratory evaluation for pediatric overweight and obesity, emphasizing age-specific guidelines and comorbidity screening.
learning_objectives: Understand age-specific diagnostic criteria for pediatric overweight and obesity using WHO and IAP charts.
Identify clinical risk factors and comorbidities associated with pediatric obesity.
Learn laboratory evaluation protocols for cardiometabolic screening in overweight and obese children.
Recognize secondary causes of obesity requiring early referral.
key_facts: WHO weight-for-length/height z-scores: Overweight > +2 SD, Obesity > +3 SD (children under 5 years).
IAP BMI-for-age charts: South Asian cutoffs are 23 (overweight risk) and 27 (obesity risk).
Screen for cardiometabolic comorbidities with fasting lipid profile, fasting glucose or HbA1c, and ALT for NAFLD.
Clinical risk factors include acanthosis nigricans, hepatomegaly, orthopedic pain, and rapid growth velocity.
Secondary causes of obesity include short stature, delayed puberty, dysmorphic features, or very rapid onset.
reference_quote: From 5–18 years, plot BMI-for-age on IAP charts and use adult-equivalent cut-offs; for South Asian children, the 23 (overweight risk) and 27 (obesity risk) lines support earlier risk detection. (Source: IAP Guidelines)
document_key_topics: Clinical risk stratification in childhood obesity
